summary: Matched several previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1BA8.c, primarily event/game-mode setup and progression logic. The decomp.dev bot reported 5 new 100% matches totaling +1500 matched bytes: gm_801BE39C, gm_801BA938, gm_801BEE9C, gm_801BF3F8, and gm_801BC488; gm_801BE638 improved to 99.59%. The PR also tightened related gm prototypes and struct fields so callers pass and interpret CharacterKind, InternalStageId, stage IDs, colors, cache entries, and small integer parameters correctly.
what_changed: Replaced stubs in gm_1BA8.c with matching C for gm_801BA938, gm_801BC488, gm_801BE39C, gm_801BEE9C, and gm_801BF3F8.
Added a near-complete implementation of gm_801BE638, reported by decomp.dev as 99.59% matched.

Implemented gm_801BA938 as a preload/cache helper that copies EventData character/color entries into lbDvd game_cache, optionally writes stage_id, builds an audio preload mask from characters and stage, then calls lbAudioAx preload/update functions.
Implemented gm_801BF3F8 with the same cache/audio-mask pattern for up to four selected characters and the current stage.

Implemented event progression logic in gm_801BE39C and gm_801BE638 using Player stock/HP checks, gmMainLib event state, ftBossLib timing helpers, camera calls, background flash, audio transitions, and game speed changes.

Implemented gm_801BEE9C minor-scene setup logic, including Game & Watch special handling through gm_80164430/gm_80164504, calls into gm_16F1 helpers, and scene transition setup via gm_801A42E8/gm_801A42D4.

Tightened prototypes across gm_1601.h, gm_16F1.h, gm_1BA8.h, and callers: gm_80164504 now takes a u16; gm_80173224 and related helpers use u8 where appropriate; gm_801BEFB0 returns CharacterKind; gm_801BF648 returns int with an explicit u8 cast.

Updated callers in gmhomerun.c, gmmultiman.c, and gmvsmelee.c to save the computed u16 stage/result value in a temporary and pass it to gm_80164504 instead of calling gm_80164504 with unknown parameters.

Refined struct knowledge: EventData fields now mark x48 as InternalStageId, x4C as CharacterKind-like s8 array, x50 as character color; lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t gained xD/xE/x16 fields; PreloadCacheSceneEntry gained x5; gm_804D6900 static layout changed x4 to intptr_t and added x18.
Added include dependencies needed by matched code, notably melee/cm/camera.h, melee/ft/ftbosslib.h, melee/ft/forward.h, and melee/gr/forward.h.
Added documentation hints that gm_8049E548_t.unk_C and gm_801BF694 relate to InternalStageId.
smart_moves: Matched cache/audio preload routines by recognizing a reusable pattern: populate lbDvd_8001822C()->game_cache, call lbDvd_80018254(), OR character masks from lbAudioAx_80026E84, OR the stage mask from lbAudioAx_80026EBC, then call lbAudioAx_80026F2C/lbAudioAx_8002702C/lbAudioAx_80027168.
Propagated the corrected gm_80164504(u16) signature into existing game-mode callers instead of leaving UNK_PARAMS, which required preserving the compared temporary value before the branch.
Used domain-specific types where evidence was strong: CharacterKind for gm_801BEFB0 and InternalStageId annotations for stage fields/returns.
Changed gm_804D6900_x4_t.x4 from int to intptr_t, allowing it to be used both as an integer-like field and pointer-cast source in matched code.
Used explicit small integer types and casts, such as gm_80173224(u8, int), gm_801736E8 all-u8 parameters, and returning (u8) gm_8049E548.c_kind[arg0] from gm_801BF648, to control extension behavior.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack/codegen details with PAD_STACK and the existing #pragma dont_inline region around gm_801BAA60.
Adjusted float literal spelling to 1.0F in several existing paths, likely to keep literal/codegen consistency with surrounding matched functions.
decomp_lessons: When a previously unknown helper gains a real signature, update all nearby callers immediately; the tmp variables in gmhomerun, gmmultiman, and gmvsmelee show how call signatures can force expression-shape changes for matching.
EventData field typing matters for downstream matches: stage IDs, character IDs, colors, and sentinel CHKIND_NONE influence both comparisons and extension behavior.
Preload cache structs may need hidden per-entry fields; adding PreloadCacheSceneEntry.x5 allowed gm_801BA938 to express a special-case write when x45 is set, i > 0, and character id is 4.

For game-mode event code, reusable terminal/cleanup sequences often include clearing xB_1, lbAudioAx_80028B90, gm_SetGameSpeed(1.0f/1.0F), gm_8016B33C, gm_8016B364, gm_8016B378, gm_8016B328, and HSD_GObjPLink_80390228.

Near matches can still be valuable when they expose struct fields and library dependencies; gm_801BE638 did not fully match but added ftBossLib/camera/game-speed behavior and state fields used by the rest of the file.
Do not over-name uncertain fields: the PR mostly keeps x-style field names while adding narrow comments only where evidence supports InternalStageId, CharacterKind, or color semantics.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names remain for unknown gm functions: gm_801BA938, gm_801BE39C, gm_801BE638, gm_801BF3F8, etc.
Static/global structs retain lbl_/gm_804D-style names and x-offset fields until better semantic names are proven.
Use project enum/type names when known: CharacterKind, InternalStageId, PlayerInitData, MinorScene, StartMeleeRules.
Comment uncertain semantic discoveries inline with Doxygen-style notes such as ///< InternalStageId, ///< @returns InternalStageId, and ///< CharacterKind rather than renaming fields prematurely.
Temporary variables in matched code may keep compiler-oriented names like temp_r31, temp_r30, var_r0, tmp, and foo when expression shape matters or semantics are not final.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(0x34), PAD_STACK(0x20), and PAD_STACK(8) to reproduce stack layout in newly matched or caller-adjusted functions.
Kept #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around an existing region, indicating inline control was matching-sensitive.
Introduced explicit temporaries for compared values before calling gm_80164504(tmp), avoiding recomputation and matching the call argument flow.
Controlled integer promotion/sign extension by changing prototypes from int to u8/s8 where needed and by returning (u8) from gm_801BF648 while exposing the return as int.
Used bool true in gm_801BA938 call sites and bool local logic in gm_801BE39C where the new signature and branch shape required it.
Added intptr_t for a static table field that is later cast as a pointer, avoiding an overly narrow int type in pointer-sensitive code.
Maintained exact float literal forms in several paths, with 0.5F and 1.0F appearing in matched boss/event timing code.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 39.15% (+0.04%, +1500 bytes).
decomp.dev bot listed 5 new 100% matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1BA8: gm_801BE39C (+636), gm_801BA938 (+296), gm_801BEE9C (+232), gm_801BF3F8 (+228), and gm_801BC488 (+108).
decomp.dev bot reported gm_801BE638 improved from 0.00% to 99.59% (+980 bytes) but was not fully matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match gm_801BE638 after this 99.59% implementation.
Search for additional gm_80164504 signature/caller cleanup now that it takes a u16.
Search for uses of EventData.x48/x4C/x50 to replace remaining x-field names with InternalStageId, CharacterKind, and color-aware names if corroborated.
Check whether PreloadCacheSceneEntry.x5 was later named and whether the arg0->x45/i>0/character id 4 special case was identified.
Compare gm_801BA938 and gm_801BF3F8 against other preload-cache/audio-mask routines for reusable decomp patterns.
